criteria: Bootloader code is exactly 512 bytes with 0x55AA signature at bytes 510-511
Bootloader successfully loads kernel binary from disk sectors starting at LBA 2 into memory at physical address 0x100000
A20 line is enabled using at least one method (BIOS INT 15h/2401, fast A20 via port 0x92, or keyboard controller) with verification test

GDT contains exactly 5 entries: null descriptor (index 0), kernel code segment (ring 0, base=0, limit=4GB, 32-bit, executable+readable), kernel data segment (ring 0, base=0, limit=4GB, writable), user code segment (ring 3), user data segment (ring 3)
GDTR is loaded with lgdt instruction before setting CR0.PE bit
Protected mode is entered by setting CR0.PE bit (bit 0 of CR0) to 1
Far jump (jmp 0x08:protected_mode_entry) is executed after CR0.PE is set to load CS with kernel code selector and flush the instruction pipeline
All data segment registers (DS, ES, FS, GS, SS) are loaded with kernel data selector (0x10) immediately after protected mode entry
32-bit stack pointer (ESP) is initialized to 0x90000 before calling kernel C code
Kernel entry point (entry.asm) zeroes the BSS section from __bss_start to __bss_end as defined in linker script
C entry point (kernel_main) is called with direction flag cleared (cld) and proper stack alignment
VGA text mode driver writes characters with color attributes to memory-mapped buffer at 0xB8000 with cursor tracking
Serial port COM1 (0x3F8) is initialized with 115200 baud, 8N1 configuration and loopback test verification
kprintf function supports format specifiers (%c, %s, %d, %i, %u, %x, %X, %p, %%) and outputs to both VGA and serial
Linker script places .text section at 0x100000 with 4KB page-aligned sections for .text, .rodata, .data, and .bss
Kernel boots successfully in QEMU and displays welcome message on both VGA console and serial output
Build system (Makefile) produces valid bootable disk image with bootloader in sector 0 and kernel starting at sector 2
Kernel entry point (_start) and C entry point (kernel_main) are properly linked with EXTERN declarations
VGA driver handles special characters (newline, carriage return, tab, backspace) and automatic scrolling
Serial driver includes transmit buffer empty check before writing each character
